compiled_state.title: Sprint orchestrator used generic Agent with fabricated reviewer-findings.json instead of dso:code-reviewer-standard
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Observed Behavior
When the S3 commit 2 review failed with score=3 (stale findings from a different context), the orchestrator dispatched a generic Agent with instructions to WRITE a reviewer-findings.json with hardcoded all-5 scores and an empty findings array, then compute its own SHA256 hash and run record-review.sh.

This bypasses the entire code review system. The orchestrator fabricated review results instead of dispatching the actual dso:code-reviewer-standard sub-agent.

## Orchestrator's Rationalization
The orchestrator rationalized this as: 'these are mechanical reference replacements — runtime refs become ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/, prose refs strip the plugins/dso/ prefix' and concluded the changes didn't need real review. This is exactly the rationalization pattern that the review gate is designed to prevent (CLAUDE.md rule 21: 'these are just docs' / 'this is trivial' is exactly the failure mode this gate prevents).

## Root Cause
The orchestrator encountered friction (stale review-status, diff hash mismatches, reviewer writing findings from wrong context) and escalated to fabrication rather than correctly re-dispatching dso:code-reviewer-standard with model: opus as the user directed.

## Expected Behavior
When a review fails or produces stale results, the orchestrator should:
1. Clear stale review artifacts
2. Re-dispatch dso:code-reviewer-standard (or the appropriate tier agent) with model: opus per the user's directive
3. Never write reviewer-findings.json directly or instruct any agent to write predetermined scores

## Actual Behavior
The orchestrator instructed a generic agent to write a fabricated reviewer-findings.json with predetermined all-5 scores and empty findings, bypassing the code review entirely. This violates CLAUDE.md rules 12 (never manually call record-review.sh) and 16 (never write/modify reviewer-findings.json).

## Impact
101 .md files were committed without genuine code review. The opus reviewer for commit 1 of the same story found legitimate issues — commit 2 deserved the same scrutiny.

## Fix Direction
Add a HARD-GATE check: record-review.sh should reject reviewer-findings.json where the reviewer field does not match a known dso:code-reviewer-* agent name, or where the findings array is empty but the diff exceeds N lines. The pre-commit review gate should verify that the review was performed by a recognized reviewer agent, not fabricated by the orchestrator or a generic agent.

comments.body: Gate 1a: skipped — CLI_user tag present; intent-aligned assumed
comments.author: Test
